data.title: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust files for IPO
data.description:
A newly formed company, Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust, filed a prospectus for an IPO on Friday. The new entity is " focused on acquiring and owning mission-critical data center assets that power the modern digital economy," it said.

The company intends to qualify as a real estate investment trust but is currently considered a "blind pool," as it hasn't yet acquired any data center assets. It expects to trade under the BXDC ticker.

The company will be externally managed by BX REIT, an affiliate of Blackstone (BX), an investment manager with $1.3T of assets under management as of Dec. 31, 2025. Blackstone Real Estate, founded in 1991, is the world's largest owner of commercial real estate, with $319B of investor capital under management as of the end of 2025. The infrastructure group was founded in 2017 and manages $77B of capital across digital infrastructure, energy, and transportation assets as of Dec. 31, 2025. 

BXDC plans to invest in digital infrastructure primarily in top data center markets that exhibit strong supply-demand dynamics. Furthermore, it will aim to acquire assets with fixed, annual rent escalators and favorable tenant expense reimbursement structures to generate predictable yields and stable cash flows.

Blackstone estimates the industry represents a substantial investment opportunity with an estimated $1T total addressable stabilized data center market expected over the next five years. 

An affiliate of Blackstone (BX) has indicated interest in buying an undisclosed number of shares in the new REIT, the filing said.

The joint lead book-running managers include Goldman Sachs (GS), Citigroup (C), and Morgan Stanley (MS).

Bloomberg News reported earlier that Blackstone was planning a $2B IPO  for an acquisition company that will buy data centers. The filing  had no estimates for the potential amount of proceeds from the proposed offering.

data.title: Lotus outlines Canada dealer expansion to 12 by year-end as For Me Eletre X global rollout starts in October
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lotus Technology Inc. (LOT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Feng Qingfeng framed 2025 as a strategic inflection point, saying, "2025 was a really important year for us, a true turning point in our strategic transformation," while emphasizing execution under tariffs and competition.
  • Feng highlighted a $23 million strategic equity investment from ECARX, saying, "we secured a strategic equity investments of $23 million from ECARX, deepening our global strategic partnership," and added, "we will jointly accelerate innovation in next-gen intelligent cockpit ecosystems to deliver AI-driven experiences."
  • Feng said Lotus ended 2025 with "211 sales outlets worldwide" and described planned channel changes, including, "we are closing a few underperforming stores, expanding the high-performing ones and redirecting resources to the markets that matter most."
  • On North America, Feng stated, "we have 6 dealers in Canada. We are expected to expand to 12 by the end of the year," adding, "We will start customer deliveries in Canada in May."
  • CFO Daxue Wang reported the quarter’s operating trends and product mix, including, "In the fourth quarter, the company delivered 1,908 vehicles," and stated, "Gross margin improved significantly to 10% in the fourth quarter compared to negative 11% in the same period of 2024."

Outlook

  • Management centered 2026 growth expectations on the PHEV launch cadence: CFO Wang said, "Going forward, we expect the global launch of our PHEV model For Me to drive sales and revenue growth," and added, "we expect to maintain the overall production pricing at current levels leading to further gross margin improvement."
  • CEO Feng laid out EU timing for For Me (Eletre X): "Wholesale deliveries in the EU start at the end of October," with "orders opening in October, official launch in November and deliveries in December," while noting, "In the U.K., we expect the wholesale to begin in mid-2027."
  • Compared with Q3, management moved from previewing a Q1 China hybrid launch to reporting it has already launched and begun delivering: Feng said, "in the first quarter of this year, we launched our all-new hybrid SUV For Me Eletre X and the delivery started just 1 day after the launch."

Financial Results

  • CFO Wang reported $163 million of Q4 revenue and $519 million for full-year 2025, and said full-year service revenue "surged 69% year-over-year to $56 million," while "Sales of goods fell 48% year-over-year to $563 million."
  • Wang attributed profitability improvement to mix, inventory, and cost actions, stating, "Gross margin improved significantly to 10%" in Q4 and citing "disciplined cost control," while also reporting, "Operating loss narrowed by 65% year-over-year to $66 million in the fourth quarter."
  • On costs, Wang said full-year R&D expense was "$171 million for the full year, down from $275 million," and added that selling and marketing fell to "$153 million from $322 million," with G&A down to "$136 million from $227 million."

Q&A

  • Xinran Li, Deutsche Bank AG, Research Division: asked what drove the 2025 delivery decline and geopolitical impact; CEO Feng: said "uncertainty of the tariffs" hurt production and inventory, added "the U.S. tariff to U.K. made vehicles affected our volumes about 60%," and said inventory was reduced "by 43% to a very healthy level" while highlighting opportunities including Canada’s EV tariff change and a "good window opportunity" for EU PHEV launches.
  • Xinran Li, Deutsche Bank AG, Research Division: asked for For Me order and positioning update; CEO Feng: said post-launch visibility improved, including "Our consumer assets have been increased by 5x," and positioned PHEV as a portfolio balancer, citing EU tariff differences: "the tariff for Chinese made PHEV in EU is 10% and for Chinese made EV is 28.8%."
  • Brian Lantier, Zacks Small-Cap Research: asked about gross margin drivers, recurrence, and 2026 direction; CFO Wang: cited "a higher production of new vehicle sales and a significant reduction in overall variable sales subsidies" plus "high-margin service revenue," while flagging "continued price increase for core components like batteries and chips" and saying a "ongoing merger with the U.K. local cars is expected to enhance the production and R&D efficiency."
  • Brian Lantier, Zacks Small-Cap Research: asked if 2025 cost cuts are sustainable; CFO Wang: said the plan is "structural long-term initiatives" across R&D leverage, marketing efficiency, and administrative streamlining, adding, "we believe these factors will continue to play a positive role in 2026."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: slightly negative, focusing on the delivery decline, geopolitics, and whether margin gains are repeatable, with questions centered on "main drivers" of volume decline and "How recurring" service-driven margin is.
  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks and generally confident in Q&A, with Feng saying, "we are pretty confident that 2026 is going to be a year for Lotus to recover," while Wang balanced optimism with headwinds such as "price increase for core components like batteries and chips."
  • Versus Q3, management appeared more execution-forward (launch and delivery started) rather than preview-oriented (upcoming hybrid), while analysts remained focused on tariffs and margin durability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 emphasized the For Me launch having occurred and provided a detailed EU commercialization calendar, while Q3 focused on a forthcoming "technology preview event in January" and a hybrid "set to launch in China" in Q1.
  • Q4 added channel metrics and near-term Canada expansion ("6 dealers" to "12") plus start-of-deliveries timing, while Q3 highlighted broader store footprint ("213 retail stores") and the Europe HQ relocation.
  • Q4 guidance language leaned toward margin progression via pricing stability and efficiency, whereas Q3 characterized gross margin as "high single-digit" and tied uplift to PHEV launch and U.K. integration benefits.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management repeatedly cited tariffs and access constraints: CEO Feng said EU and U.S. tariffs "exerted pressure on our pricing" and that in the U.S. BEV market "it is impossible for us to enter."
  • CFO Wang flagged cost inflation risk, citing "continued price increase for core components like batteries and chips," and positioned mitigation around procurement and efficiency, including expectations that "total procurement costs, production costs and unit D&A" will decline.
  • CEO Feng also noted oil-price-related pressure points, saying supply chain cost and "the bond cost might be also increased correspondingly."

Final Takeaway

Management portrayed Q4 2025 as a margin-recovery and cost-discipline period while shifting the 2026 narrative to execution on the For Me (Eletre X) PHEV rollout, including EU wholesale timing beginning at the end of October and a planned expansion of Canada dealers to 12 by year-end, with tariffs, component inflation, and market-access constraints remaining central risks discussed throughout the call.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Arqit Quantum sees H1 FY2026 revenue at $0.6M
data.description:
• Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) said on Friday it expects preliminary H1 FY2026 revenue of $0.62M–$0.63M, compared with $0.07M for the same period in fiscal year 2025 and $0.5M for FY 2025.
  • Cash and cash equivalents had stood at about $28.9M as of March 31, 2026.
  • Revenue growth had been driven by recognition from existing contracts and three new deals signed during the period.
  • The consensus estimates for FY2026 are revenue of $2.48M and EPS of -$1.52

data.title: A software reckoning is coming
data.description:
The software sector (IGV), (XSW) continues to bleed as investors flee to semiconductors (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (XSD), and other sectors, but Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research believes a turning point is near.

“A rip-roaring bully-o-bull week in the S&P (SP500), the Nasdaq (COMP:IND), the Russell (IWM), crypto, semiconductors (SMH), and plenty more besides,” King wrote. “The Dow (DJI) lagged the other indices, and software (IGV) continued to be a source of funds as the Claude-Is-Eating-The-World narrative crushes everything before it. For now.”

But he believes “a reckoning is coming in software, with a number of names poised to surprise to the upside come Q1 earnings.”

King argued that enterprise software companies now face a stark choice. CEOs can either ignore the AI transformation—a strategy King calls “dumb” that will lead to slow atrophy and eventual acquisition by private equity firms—or “choose violence” through aggressive restructuring.

The violent path, King explains, means deploying zero-day budgeting and hollowing out workforces. He predicts companies pursuing this route could deliver revenue growth exceeding 20% annually with cash flow margins of 50% or better.

He also estimates fully one-third of staff at these companies could be cut, with AI tools replacing expensive developers and slashing stock-based compensation.

The bearish sentiment on software is well-documented. Citi Research recently downgraded six application software stocks to Neutral, including Docusign (DOCU), Autodesk (ADSK), and Veeva Systems (VEEV), citing intensifying AI concerns.

The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has fallen to levels not seen since November 2023, down more than 27% in 2026.

However, former JPMorgan strategist Marko Kolanovic noted the divergence between software and semiconductors has become “so statistically extreme” that a short-term bounce may be overdue, suggesting a potential tactical opportunity for contrarian investors.

data.title: Tariff refund tool will launch on April 20, US Customs says
data.description:
Importers seeking tariff refunds will be able to submit requests starting April 20, the U.S. Customs and Border Protection ((CBP)) said on Friday.

The electronic application process comes two months after the  Supreme Court ruled that President Donald Trump exceeded his authority in using the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to impose sweeping tariffs on goods from almost every trading partner.

The Consolidated and Processing of Entries ((CAPE)) tool in the Automated Commercial Environment Secure Data Portal (ACE Portal) is designed to simplify IEEPA duty refund requests by providing an electronic pathway to submit claims, the U.S. CBP said.

Once claims are accepted, the CBP will remove the IEEPA Harmonized Tariff Schedule number and recalculate the duties due without IEEPA, updating the entry to a new version. CBP will review the updated version of the entry and liquidate or reliquidate, it said in its notice.

Importers of record and customs brokers will need to have an  ACE Portal account to file claims through the tool.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: IM Cannabis receives Nasdaq notice over minimum bid price requirement
data.description:
• IM Cannabis (IMCC) on Friday said that it has received a notice from Nasdaq indicating it is not in compliance with the exchange’s minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company said it has 180 calendar days, until October 6, 2026, to regain compliance by maintaining a closing bid price of at least $1.00 per share for a minimum of 10 consecutive business days.
  • IM Cannabis said the notice has no immediate impact on its Nasdaq listing or trading, and its shares will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • IMCC closed +1.66% at $0.3609.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. stocks take weekly gains despite continued Middle East conflict
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages struggled on Friday, with both the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Dow ((DJI)) in the red, as the core retail inflation print for March came in slightly cooler than expected, while investors continued to brace for U.S.-Iran war tensions.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.1%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +0.4%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished -0.6%.

Week-to-date, however, all indexes took home gains. The S&P 500 was +3.6%, the Nasdaq was +4.7%, and the Dow was +5.5%.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields remained steady. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 4 basis points higher at 4.32%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) also rose 4 basis points to 3.81%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were higher at $95.9, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $94.5 per barrel.

“A rip-roaring bully-o-bull week in the S&P, the Nasdaq, the Russell, crypto, semiconductors, and plenty more besides,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research.

“The Dow lagged the other indices, and software continued to be a source of funds as the Claude-Is-Eating-The-World narrative crushed everything before it. For now,” King added. “We believe a reckoning is coming in software, with a number of names poised to surprise to the upside come Q1 earnings.”

In economic news, the March U.S. core Consumer Price Index, which excludes food and energy, came in weaker at +0.2% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus. Headline CPI, including food and energy prices, was +0.9% MoM, in line with consensus.

In addition, the Consumer Sentiment Index came in lower at 47.6 for April vs. the 52.0 consensus, according to preliminary data by the University of Michigan Survey.

Lastly, the February factory orders were unchanged MoM at $619.6B vs. the +0.4% consensus, the Census Bureau said.

The uncertainty surrounding a potential truce between the U.S., Israel, and Iran remained at the forefront for traders. President Donald Trump said on Friday that U.S. warships are being reloaded with “the best weapons ever made” to resume strikes on Iran if peace talks in Pakistan fail.

On Thursday, Trump warned that Iran should not be charging fees to oil tankers traveling through the Strait of Hormuz. “If they are, they better stop now!” he said on Truth Social.

data.title: Semiconductor stocks lead Nasdaq this week
data.description:
The Nasdaq Composite Index (COMP:IND) ended in the green on Friday for the second straight week, with semiconductor stocks emerging as winners for the week. It closed up 0.35%.

The week’s performance showed a clear rotation within technology, with chip stocks continuing their recovery following weeks of losses amid the U.S.-Iran conflict. The top five Nasdaq 100 performers this week were from the semiconductor industry, led by Intel (INTC) and Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR). Meanwhile, application software stocks like Intuit (INTU) and Atlassian (TEAM) lost ground.

Earlier in the week, Wall Street propped up after both warring factions agreed to a conditional ceasefire. However, uncertainties continue to persist over the ceasefire agreement, keeping investors on their toes.

Top gainers of the week:

Intel (INTC) +24.55%

Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) +21.21%

Lam Research (LRCX) +20.96%

Marvell Technology (MRVL) +20.32%

Broadcom (AVGO) +18.56%

Top losers for the week:

Intuit (INTU) -16.99%

Atlassian (TEAM) -16.68%

Axon Enterprise (AXON) -16.60%

Zscaler (ZS) -15.40%

Workday (WDAY) -15.02%

data.title: Rio Tinto attracting interest for critical mineral assets in California - Bloomberg
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) has attracted interest from more than a dozen potential bidders for its U.S. assets that produce the critical mineral boron, which could fetch ~$2B in a sale, Bloomberg reported Friday.

WE Soda, the world’s largest producer of soda ash; Magris Resources, a Canadian mining investment firm; and U.S. Silica Holdings, one of the world's top producers of silica sand used in glassmaking and construction, are among the companies considering a purchase of Rio's (RIO) boron assets in California, according to the report.

Rio (RIO) has said its California operations meet about a third of global demand for refined boron, which is used in fertilizers as well as in high-tech applications such as heat-resistant glass and materials for renewable energy, and is a key to stabilizing permanent rare earth magnets.

The company's boron assets include a mine and processing operations in the Mojave Desert town of Boron, a refinery and shipping facility at the Port of Los Angeles, and its Owens Lake mining operation near the Sierra Nevada mountains.

data.title: Better-for-you beverage brand Suja Life files for an IPO
data.description:
Suja Life filed for an IPO on Friday. The company is the parent of the Suja Organic, Vive Organic, and Slice Soda brands. 

In its filing, Suja Life described itself as a vertically integrated manufacturer and marketer of organic cold-pressed juices, functional wellness shots, and other plant-based beverages, operating an in-house high-pressure processing facility and relying on a cold-chain distribution network to serve grocery, mass, natural, and club retailers nationwide. Suja Life recorded $327M in revenue for the 12 months ended December 31, 2025. 

Suja Life was founded in 2012 and evolved from a niche cold-pressed juice startup into a multi-brand platform backed by private equity firm Paine Schwartz Partners and co-investor Meaningful Partners. Subsequent acquisitions, including the purchase of wellness shot maker Vive Organic in 2022 and the revival of legacy soft drink Slice as a cleaner-label soda, are highlighted as part of a strategy to build scale in functional and premium beverages.

Looking ahead, the California-based company sees its use of organic, non-GMO, and plant-based ingredients as key points of differentiation in a crowded refrigerated beverage category. Suja plans to use net proceeds to fund brand marketing, innovation, capacity investments, and potential future acquisitions, while also strengthening its balance sheet. For prospective investors, the IPO frames Suja as a scaled, category-leading cold-chain beverage platform seeking capital to accelerate its next phase of growth in the competitive but expanding functional beverage market.

In terms of Suja Life impacting mass market brands with increased funding, the company could compete with brands owned by the major beverage groups, such as Naked Juice and Tropicana under PepsiCo (PEP), Odwalla and Simply under Coca-Cola (KO), and organic-leaning portfolios at Hain Celestial (HAIN) and Danone (DANOY).
